If no preference is registered, defaults to the global `Promise` for newer Node.js versions. The browser version defaults to the window `Promise`, so polyfill or register as necessary.
# Install preferred promise library
Register your preference in the application entry point before any other `require` of packages that load `any-promise`:
As an application author, you can *optionally* register a preferred `Promise` implementation on application startup (before any call to `require('any-promise')`:

You must register your preference before any call to `require('any-promise')` (by you or required packages), and only one implementation can be registered. Typically, this registration would occur at the top of the application entry point.
If you are using a known `Promise` implementation, you can register your preference with a shortcut:
Shortcut registration is the preferred registration method as it works in the browser and Node.js. It is also convenient for using with `import` and many test runners, that offer a `--require` flag:

As an alternative to registration shortcuts, you can call the `register` function with the preferred `Promise` implementation. The benefit of this approach is that a `Promise` library can be required by name without being a known implementation.  This approach does NOT work in the browser. To use `any-promise` in the browser use either registration shortcuts or specify the `Promise` constructor using advanced registration (see below).

Your preference will be registered globally, allowing a single registration even if multiple versions of `any-promise` are installed in the NPM dependency tree or are using multiple bundled JavaScript files in the browser. You can bypass this global registration in options:

If an implementation is not registered, `any-promise` will attempt to discover an installed `Promise` implementation.  If no implementation can be found, an error will be thrown on `require('any-promise')`.  While the auto-discovery usually avoids errors, it is non-deterministic. It is recommended that the user always register a preferred implementation for older Node.js versions.

The face detector has been trained on a custom dataset of ~14K images labeled with bounding boxes. Furthermore the model has been trained to predict bounding boxes, which entirely cover facial feature points, thus it in general produces better results in combination with subsequent face landmark detection than SSD Mobilenet V1.

The neural net is equivalent to the **FaceRecognizerNet** used in [face-recognition.js](https://github.com/justadudewhohacks/face-recognition.js) and the net used in the [dlib](https://github.com/davisking/dlib/blob/master/examples/dnn_face_recognition_ex.cpp) face recognition example. The weights have been trained by [davisking](https://github.com/davisking) and the model achieves a prediction accuracy of 99.38% on the LFW (Labeled Faces in the Wild) benchmark for face recognition.
## Face Expression Recognition Model

The face expression recognition model is lightweight, fast and provides reasonable accuracy. The model has a size of roughly 310kb and it employs depthwise separable convolutions and densely connected blocks. It has been trained on a variety of images from publicly available datasets as well as images scraped from the web. Note, that wearing glasses might decrease the accuracy of the prediction results.

For applications requiring higher precision or where working with fractions is preferable, consider incorporating *Fraction.js* into your project.
The library effectively addresses precision issues, as demonstrated below:

*Fraction.js* uses a `BigInt` representation for both the numerator and denominator, ensuring minimal performance overhead while maximizing accuracy. Its design is optimized for precision, making it an ideal choice as a foundational library for other math tools, such as [Polynomial.js](https://github.com/rawify/Polynomial.js) and [Math.js](https://github.com/josdejong/mathjs).
This is particularly useful when you need precise fraction representations instead of dealing with the limitations of floating-point arithmetic. What if you allow some error tolerance?
## Precision

As native `BigInt` support in JavaScript becomes more common, libraries like *Fraction.js* use it to handle calculations with higher precision. This improves the speed and accuracy of math operations with large numbers, providing a better solution for tasks that need more precision than floating-point numbers can offer.

While the result is reasonably close, it’s not as accurate as the fraction-based approach that *Fraction.js* provides, especially when dealing with repeating decimals or complex operations. This highlights the value of precision that the library brings.

For instance, when using the plus operator like `"123" + new Fraction`, `valueOf()` will be called first, as JavaScript attempts to combine primitives before concatenating them, with the string type taking precedence. ### POSIX bracket expressions

* Micromatch exclusively and explicitly reserves backslashes for escaping characters in a glob pattern, even on windows, which is consistent with bash behavior. _More importantly, unescaping globs can result in unsafe regular expressions_.

* Glob patterns are not filepaths. They are a type of [regular language](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_language) that is converted to a JavaScript regular expression. Thus, when forward slashes are defined in a glob pattern, the resulting regular expression will match windows or POSIX path separators just fine.

Determine if the current node version supports the `--preserve-symlinks` flag.
var supportsPreserveSymlinks = require('node-supports-preserve-symlinks-flag');
assert.equal(supportsPreserveSymlinks, null); // in a browser
assert.equal(supportsPreserveSymlinks, false); // in node < v6.2
assert.equal(supportsPreserveSymlinks, true); // in node v6.2+

1. **Lecture 02b (第 19-20 页)**：WBS（工作分解结构）可以有不同的表现形式。课件中提到了两种主要的格式：图形树格式（Graphical Tree format）和轮廓/文本列表格式（Outline format，带有小数编号的系统）
2. **基于产品 (Product WBS)**：以实体为导向（Entity-oriented），例如按前端页面、后端接口、数据库等物理模块来划分 。
3. **基于过程/阶段 (Process WBS)**：以活动为导向（Activity-oriented），按项目生命周期阶段来划分，例如需求分析、设计、开发、测试等 。
1. **Lecture 02b (第 30-31 页)**：WBS 词典用于详细描述 WBS 中包含的元素（即工作包）的详细内容 。简单的词典描述了该元素**包含什么**，以及**不包含什么** 。复杂的还可以包括负责组织、质量要求、时间表等 。
# 第四次作业
- **情景脚本数量 (Number of scenario scripts)**：情景脚本是关于用户与应用程序交互的详细步骤序列 。情景脚本的数量与应用程序的规模以及测试用例的数量直接相关。
- **比例关系**：用例的数量与应用程序的代码行数 (LOC) 规模成正比 ，同时也与将要设计的测试用例数量成正比 。

# PsySight WBS Dictionary
This document provides a structured WBS Dictionary for the **PsySight Intelligent Psychological Support System**, based on:
- The **Deliverable-Oriented WBS** (product/subsystem view).
- The **Process-Oriented WBS** (lifecycle/phase view).
Each level-3 WBS element includes the following fields:
## Section A. Deliverable-Oriented WBS Dictionary

Allow logged-in users to view and update key personal information (nickname, gender, age, region, preferences) to enable more contextual assessments and anonymized statistics while preserving privacy.
- Store user demographic fields and display preferences in the `User` model.
- Updated user records reflecting demographic and preference changes.
- Store anonymity flags and stats-visibility preferences alongside assessment records.
- Context-aware AI replies that reflect previous user messages within the session.
- UI that shows previous messages and sessions clearly to users.
- Returning to a past session shows previously stored messages correctly.
Detect crisis-related expressions in user messages and surface immediate, non-intrusive rescue information such as hotlines and emergency guidance.
- Validate answers and prevent accidental submissions (anti-misclick measures).
### 1.4 Creative Expression Subsystem
Provide a playful, low-pressure drawing canvas for students to express themselves visually as part of projective assessment.
Extract human-understandable metadata from the drawing session (e.g., color usage, completion hints, presence of typical elements) to provide structured context for AI interpretation.
- Domain assumptions about what metadata is meaningful yet privacy-preserving.
Use an AI model to interpret drawing metadata and reflection text to generate gentle, projective feedback that helps students reflect on their emotional state.
- Call DeepSeek (or compatible) API with `meta_summary` and reflection text to request interpretive feedback.
- On-screen projective interpretation visible to users.
- Submitting a drawing reliably returns an AI interpretation within acceptable time.
Present anonymous group-level emotional and assessment statistics on a visual dashboard to give users and instructors an overview of current trends.
- Layout and colors remain readable for classroom presentation.
## Section B. Process-Oriented WBS Dictionary
- Articulate project purpose across chat, assessments, and creative expression.
- Initial scope baseline and user stories (e.g., from “lingge” narratives).
- Backlog items cover all major WBS deliverables.
#### 1.1.3 Develop Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM)
- **Name**: Develop Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM)
Map user stories and requirements to specific system components, WBS items, and acceptance tests to ensure coverage and avoid gaps.
- Define a mapping structure between user stories, WBS IDs, and modules (frontend, backend, docs).
- User stories (e.g., from “lingge” documents).
- WBS structure (Deliverable- and Process-Oriented).
- RTM document or table linking requirements to implementation and test artifacts.
- Each core requirement can be traced to at least one WBS item and implemented module.
- Reports include score interpretation and gentle advice, with disclaimers.
Formalize and implement a lexicon-based engine that detects high-risk expressions and routes them to proper crisis handling flows.
- DeepSeek API for interpretation.
- Students can draw, submit, and receive interpretation within a single session.
- Prepare manual smoke test checklist for front-to-back flows.
#### 1.6.2 Course Project UAT (User Acceptance Testing) Acceptance
- **Name**: Course Project UAT (User Acceptance Testing) Acceptance
- Record and track UAT issues and resolutions.
- Backlog and RTM (where available).
- UAT feedback notes and a simple sign-off indicating readiness for defense.
#### 1.6.3 Prepare Course Defense Demo (Presentation)
- **Name**: Prepare Course Defense Demo (Presentation)
Create a coherent and time-bounded demo plan and presentation that clearly communicates PsySight’s goals, architecture, and key features.
- Prepare slides summarizing problem, solution, architecture, and highlights.
- Architecture and WBS documentation (including this dictionary).
- Product Owner, Presenting Team Members.
- Demo can be executed without major hiccups in a typical classroom environment.

hits.text: # PsySight 第一次课程展示演讲成稿
## 第 1 人：项目背景、项目定义、Scope Statement
**建议界面**: `[PPT 封面 -> 项目说明书摘要页]`
大家好，我们是 `PsySight` 小组。我们这次展示的项目，是一个面向大学生的智能心理支持系统。之所以做这个项目，是因为大学生群体中普遍存在焦虑、失眠、情绪低落、压力积累等问题，但现实中常见的方案往往有两个割裂的极端：要么只是聊天陪伴，缺少客观评估；要么只是做量表，缺少情绪支持和后续建议。
从项目定义上看，`PsySight` 的目标用户是大学生；项目目标是在课程周期内交付一个可运行的 Web 系统，让用户能够完成 AI 倾诉、量表评测、AI 报告查看，以及匿名统计共鸣等核心流程。我们的主要交付物包括前后端系统本身、量表库与报告链路、项目文档、GitHub 仓库、领歌中的过程记录，以及最终的演示成果。
同时，这个项目也有明确约束和假设。约束包括课程时间有限、团队规模只有五人、系统实现需要兼顾隐私和可用性；假设则包括用户愿意匿名或登录使用、外部 AI 服务可用、课程展示阶段以 Web 主链路为主要交付形式。我们的验收标准也比较明确：核心链路必须能跑通，危机提醒必须能触发，报告必须能生成，项目过程必须能在 GitHub 和领歌中被追踪。
接下来，第 2 位成员会继续从项目管理的角度，说明我们如何用 `5W2H`、`RTM` 和 `WBS` 把这个项目从想法真正落成一个可执行的软件工程计划。
## 第 2 人：5W2H、RTM、WBS、优先级管理
在项目定义之后，我们进一步用 `5W2H` 来让范围和执行方式更具体。
地点上，团队成员每天给我汇报，主要通过图书馆和线上交流。
范围和执行方式确认之后，我们分析了我们的需求。这个矩阵列出了我们的五大需求。我们需要使用RTM，追踪、验证这些需求。
第一个RTM列出了需求是如何对应WBS交付物和产品设计的。第二个RTM列出了需求如何对应产品开发和测试项目。然后我们对第三个需求进一步分解，展示不同的需求点是如何联系前端设计和后端接口。
在工作分解上，我们采用了两种 `WBS`，第一种是按产品拆解，也就是把系统分成用户管理，聊天系统，报告分析，多模态系统和数据可视化五个部分。
针对这两种WBS，我们构建了交付物字典。我们将每个需求都拆解，定义。每个需求都包含以上元素，如右图所示。
接下来，第 3 位成员会继续说明我们如何用 `WBS Dictionary` 和 `领歌` 把这些拆解真正变成可执行、可追踪的任务体系。
## 第 3 人：WBS Dictionary、领歌展示、Scrum 推进逻辑
**建议界面**: `[切到领歌 Backlog / 迭代页 -> 打开一个典型任务卡]`

前面已经讲到，我们不仅做了工作分解，还进一步补了 `WBS Dictionary`，也就是对工作包做更清楚的边界说明。这里我们以“专业评测模块”为例。这个工作包包含量表库浏览、题目加载、作答进度、提交评分、报告生成等内容；不包含复杂的量表后台编辑和发布工作流；它的验收标准则是用户能够顺利进入量表、完成作答、生成结果，并且看到分级和建议。通过这种方式，工作包不再只是一个标题，而是有了清楚的边界、责任和完成标准。
接下来我们展示 `领歌`。这里请老师重点看三个层次。第一层是需求池和迭代划分，我们把项目按 `M1` 到 `M4` 分阶段推进，不同阶段关注的目标不同。第二层是任务优先级和状态流转，可以看到哪些任务属于核心必须项，哪些是增强项，以及它们如何从待办进入进行中再到完成。第三层是任务卡本身，里面会有用户故事、描述、验收条件等信息，这说明我们的项目管理不是“想到什么做什么”，而是围绕可验收的任务推进。
从方法上说，我们采用的是 `Scrum Model` 风格的敏捷开发模式。也就是说，我们先建立产品待办列表，再按阶段选择最关键的故事进入当前迭代，然后围绕验收条件完成开发、测试和演示准备。对于课程项目来说，这种方式比一次性把所有功能摊开更适合，因为它能帮助团队在有限时间里先保证高优先级功能稳定落地。
领歌在这里承担的作用，不是代替系统本身，而是证明这个项目有真实的项目管理过程：有计划、有拆解、有优先级、有推进节奏，也有验收依据。这一点和前面的 `RTM`、`WBS` 一起，构成了我们范围管理和过程管理的核心证据。
接下来，第 4 位成员会通过一条完整的 `Demo` 主链路，展示这些需求和计划是如何真正落到系统中的。
## 第 4 人：Demo 主链路讲解
**建议界面**: `[播放 Demo 录屏]`
接下来我用一条最核心的主链路，来展示 `PsySight` 已经如何从需求走向落地。这里我们不打算把所有功能都演一遍，而是集中展示最能覆盖评分点的闭环。
提交量表之后，系统会先给出一个鼓励性的过渡界面，再进入报告页。报告中会展示总分、严重程度分级、AI 生成的解释与建议，以及免责声明。如果分数达到高风险区间，系统还会优先提示专业求助建议。也就是说，报告页不仅告诉用户“结果是什么”，还会告诉用户“这意味着什么、下一步可以做什么”。
如果时间允许，我们还可以补充展示绘画投射功能，说明系统除了文本交互和量表评测之外，还为不善言辞的用户提供了非语言表达路径。但在本次汇报中，我们把主时间留给最核心的端到端闭环，也就是 `聊天 -> 推荐 -> 测评 -> 报告 -> 统计联动`。
## 第 5 人：GitHub、软件度量、FP、COCOMO II、决策树、总结
**建议界面**: `[切到 GitHub 仓库首页 -> docs/architecture-overview.md -> docs/product-backlog.md -> docs/refactor-baseline-gate.md -> 回 PPT 总结页]`
最后，我从工程化和软件工程分析的角度做总结。首先看 `GitHub`。我们展示 GitHub，不是为了证明“我们写了很多代码”，而是为了证明这个项目有清晰的交付物和可追踪的工程资产。比如在仓库里可以看到架构总览文档、产品 backlog、启动与收口说明、重构基线门禁等内容。这说明我们的项目不是临时拼接的 Demo，而是有范围说明、过程文档、技术结构和质量门禁的完整工程项目。
最后，我们想用一句话总结这次展示：`PsySight` 不只是一个可以运行的课程作品，而是一个有明确问题定义、有范围和需求管理、有敏捷推进过程、有端到端落地结果，也有工程化文档和度量估算支撑的软件工程项目。谢谢老师和同学们。
我们后续会继续完善测试覆盖、性能优化和更多量表能力，但在当前阶段，我们已经优先完成了课程最核心的项目闭环与管理证据。

## 已确认的展示约束
- 展示必须覆盖课程要求中的核心评分项：项目定义、`Scope Statement`、`5W2H`、`RTM`、`WBS`、`WBS Dictionary`、软件度量、`FP/LOC` 选择、`COCOMO II`、决策树、`领歌`。
- 展示中还必须包含 `GitHub` 展示、`领歌` 展示、项目 `Demo` 录屏。

# PsySight 第一次课程展示规划
围绕老师在 `[D:/100-code/110-Project/PsySight/docs/pre/课后作业统计.md](D:/100-code/110-Project/PsySight/docs/pre/课后作业统计.md)` 中对应的全部得分点，结合课程课件 `docs/class` 与项目现有真实材料，整理一套：

- 每一部分的核心得分点、展示证据、避坑提醒
- 输出到 `docs/pre`，并按你的规则补上创建/更新时间
- `GitHub`、`领歌`、`Demo录屏` 不单独割裂展示，而是嵌入总流程中承担不同评分点。
- 领歌部分按“现有页面尽量贴合”设计，不虚构你们没有的成员协作细节。
- 评分要求主依据：`[D:/100-code/110-Project/PsySight/docs/pre/课后作业统计.md](D:/100-code/110-Project/PsySight/docs/pre/课后作业统计.md)`
- 工程化门禁与测试证据：`[D:/100-code/110-Project/PsySight/docs/refactor-baseline-gate.md](D:/100-code/110-Project/PsySight/docs/refactor-baseline-gate.md)`
- 关键展示主链路：聊天 -> 量表推荐 -> 作答 -> 报告 -> 统计看板 -> 紧急求助/隐私控制
- 5W2H
- RTM、WBS 两种结构、WBS dictionary
- 领歌
用时间切片组织整场展示，优先保证高分项先讲、低风险素材先展示：
opening[项目定位与目标] --> scope5w2h[项目说明书+5W2H]
scope5w2h --> management[RTM+WBS+领歌]
management --> demoFlow[Demo主链路]
- 第 1 人：项目背景、项目定义、Scope Statement、5W2H 的 Why/What
- 第 2 人：5W2H 的 When/Who/How/How much，接 RTM 与 WBS

- 建议展示的页面/截图/录屏点位
在 `docs/pre` 下整理最终文档，计划包含：
- 一份总规划：展示顺序、时间分配、得分点映射、各环节展示素材
- 领歌部分不编造“已有成员分工”，而是强调阶段划分、用户故事、优先级、验收条件与推进逻辑。
- Demo 只保留最能覆盖评分点的一条主链路，不追求展示所有功能。
- GitHub 展示重点放在文档体系、分层架构、基线门禁与冒烟验证，不把希望压在提交记录上。
